title: Plum frangipane tart
subtitle: Quartered plums on almond cream in a sweet shortcrust
excerpt: Autumn plums quartered and pressed cut-side up into almond frangipane in a sweet shortcrust shell. The plums weep their juice into the frangipane and turn deep purple in the bake. One 23 cm tart, serving eight.
sourceNotes: Eliza Acton, Modern Cookery for Private Families (1845), Project Gutenberg: documents the British plum-frangipane tart in the autumn-fruit section. Marie-Antoine Carême, L'Art de la cuisine française (1833), Project Gutenberg: documents the French frangipane form. Mrs Beeton, Book of Household Management (1861), Project Gutenberg #10136: documents the use of stone fruit pressed into almond cream as a household tart shape.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze the baked tart whole, wrapped; defrost overnight in the fridge.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Pastry 2 days ahead; frangipane mixed on the day of baking.

content.text: A plum frangipane tart is built in three layers: a blind-baked sweet shortcrust shell, a frangipane filling (almond cream of butter, sugar, eggs, ground almonds and a little flour), and quartered plums pressed cut-side up into the frangipane before baking. The plums sink slightly into the cream and stain it with their juice; the surface darkens to deep gold as the bake finishes.

Victoria plums, damsons, mirabelles and Marjorie's Seedlings all work; small plums halved, larger plums quartered. Damsons need an extra teaspoon of sugar in the frangipane; mirabelles and Victorias usually do not.

items.prepNote: stoned and quartered (or halved if small)

content.text: Rub the cold first {{unsalted-butter}} into the {{plain-flour}}, {{icing-sugar}} and {{sea-salt-fine}}. Bind with the yolk of the first {{eggs}} and {{water}}. Chill an hour. Heat the oven to 180°C fan. Roll, line a 23 cm tart tin, trim, prick. Blind-bake with beans for 15 minutes, remove beans and bake another 5 minutes.

Beat the softened second {{unsalted-butter}} with the second {{caster-sugar}} until pale and creamed. Beat in the two {{eggs}}. Fold in the {{almond-flour}}, second {{plain-flour}} and {{almond-extract}} for the frangipane. Spread evenly into the warm pastry case. Press the quartered {{plums}} cut-side up across the surface, packing them in. Scatter the {{almonds-flaked}} and {{demerara-sugar}} over.

Bake for 35–40 minutes; the frangipane should be set and deep gold around the plums, the plums should look slightly burnished, and a skewer through the frangipane should come out clean. Cool fully in the tin before slicing.
